content:
This article looks into how LLMs function at a high-level — Going from how they are trained to the transformer architecture.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Jona on UnsplashIntroduction
LLMs are one of the driving forces behind the AI revolution — from coding agents to contextually aware retrieval generation. As of mid-2026, they power the world’s most popular AI applications.
Being able to build a large language model from scratch is arguably one of the most important technical skills anyone looking to consolidate in the AI field could have.
I’ve figured that writing about building an LLM from scratch in a 7-article series is perhaps the best way I have to force myself to think deeply about it and to help others in their journey through AI mastery.
We start with some ground-covering ideas that will hopefully get you started with LLMs. By the end of this article, you will have gained a working understanding of concepts such as pretraining, fine-tuning, and the transformer architecture.
At the end of the article, we will lay out a plan for how we might go about creating a language model from scratch.
